rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN L. VALENTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests an order that 1/2 of the subject properties pass to Petition as the surviving spouse; however, Decedent's will did not leave any of his asset to her as an individual. Petitioner avers that terms of Decedent's will do not control the distribution of this property, as it was held in Petitioner's sole name; however, Petitioner does not provide authority for this conclusion and does not allege how property should pass. 2) Petitioner states that if the Court is unwilling to issue an order addressing the transfer of Decedent's interest to her, then she requests an order confirming that the accounts are community property. However, spousal property petitions are not used to determine solely the character of property. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GLORIA ANNE BARRACK TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Personal Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/24/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The amended petition was file stamped on 3/7/22, but not actually e-file until 3/9/22, when it became available for viewing. In any event, notice mailed is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Prob. C. Sect. 850 succeeded Heggstad and sets higher standards for proving that assets not transferred to a trust during a trustor's life were, in fact, intended to be held in the trust. In other words, a beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect, 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] How are the assets at issue currently titled? Please file the bonds or the account(s) holding the bonds and the Wells Fargo Bank statement immediately preceding the date of death. Did the settlor leave a written list for the disposition of tangible personal property? Any supplement or amendment must be filed in time to serve a copy of it to all of the interested parties at least 30 days before the continued hearing, after the supplement or amendment is filed. [CRC 7.53(b).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/13/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/24/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The trustee resides in Florida. How is this the proper venue? Did the trustor have a pour-over will or a general assignment? There was no specifically described real property on the schedule of assets to the incomplete trust dated 3/7/1991 and no schedule of assets at all to the amended and restated trust dated 3/26/10. The deeds, furthermore, refer to a trust dated 3/18/98. Any supplement or amendment must be filed in time to serve a copy of it to all of the interested parties at least 30 days before the continued hearing, after the supplement or amendment is filed. [CRC 7.53(b).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/13/22. (Only one copy is required to be provided.) rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEVIN TYLER MCATEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The Court is to review the sufficiency of additional evidence submitted in support of the decedent's domicile in San Francisco at the time of his death. 2) The petitioner is a friend of the decedent. The decedent's father, as the sole heir, nominated the petitioner as administrator and waived his bond. The estimated value of the estate solely consisting of personal property is $1,000,000. The Court is to determine whether a bond should be waived, if the petitioner is to be appointed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA ROSE NAKANO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/24/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No proof of service. No proof of publication. No Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities. No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License (filed conventionally and confidentially). The petition caption is incomplete if the petitioner seeks full IAEA authority. Item 3g(2)(c) should have been answered, as the petitioner is reportedly a son. Items 5a(3) or (4) and (7) or (8) and 5b of the petition must be answered. No waivers of bond were attached to the petition or have been filed separately. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/13/22 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALINDE MARIE O'MALLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/03/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3) Please state Decedent's parents names and their date of death. 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
